stem: A clinical research study measures treatment response rate across 3 trial sites, each containing multiple patient cohorts. trial site A has 15 observations with mean 50.0 % (within-trial site SD = 4.9). trial site B has 5 observations with mean 78.3 % (within-trial site SD = 9.1). trial site C has 8 observations with mean 47.4 % (within-trial site SD = 7.5). The between-trial site standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.7 and 16.8 %. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true treatment response rate for trial site C, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-trial site variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for trial site C: 47.8 % (shrunk from raw 47.4). Range [47.6, 48.3] as between-trial site SD varies from 6.7 to 16.8. Pooled mean: 54.3.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 54.3 % (weighted average of 3 trial site means).
Shrinkage factor for trial site C: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/8). At σ_between=11.2: B = 0.947.
Interval [47.6, 48.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (47.4) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (54.3) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
